question.english: What factors should be taken into account when making advertisements?
question.chinese: 製作廣告時，應該考慮哪些因素？

source_lines.text: Topic: What factors should be taken into account when making advertisements?
製作廣告時，應該考慮哪些因素？

components.english: I think one of the most important factors is the target audience, because an advertisement only works well when it speaks to the right people in the right way.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個最重要的因素就是目標受眾，因為一則廣告只有在用對的方式對準對的人說話時，才真正有效。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the most important factors is the target audience, because
an advertisement only works well when it speaks to the right people in the right
way.
[我認為，其中一個最重要的因素就是目標受眾，因為一則廣告只有在用對的方式對
準對的人說話時，才真正有效。]

components.english: Before making any advertisement, companies need to think about who they are trying to reach, what those people care about, and what kind of message is likely to resonate with them. The tone, language, visuals, and even the platform all depend on that. After all, an ad that tries to speak to everyone often ends up speaking to no one. If the message misses the mark, the whole campaign can feel like a shot in the dark.
components.chinese: 在製作任何廣告之前，公司都需要先想清楚，他們想接觸的是誰、那些人在乎甚麼，以及甚麼樣的訊息最容易打動他們。廣告的語氣、用字、畫面，甚至投放的平台，其實都取決於這一點。畢竟，一則想對所有人說話的廣告，最後往往反而誰都說服不了。如果訊息沒有打中重點，整個宣傳活動就很容易像盲目亂投一樣。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Before making any advertisement, companies need to think
about who they are trying to reach, what those people care about, and what
kind of message is likely to resonate with them. The tone, language, visuals, and
even the platform all depend on that. After all, an ad that tries to speak to
everyone often ends up speaking to no one. If the message misses the mark, the
whole campaign can feel like a shot in the dark.
[在製作任何廣告之前，公司都需要先想清楚，他們想接觸的是誰、那些人在乎甚麼，
以及甚麼樣的訊息最容易打動他們。廣告的語氣、用字、畫面，甚至投放的平台，其實
都取決於這一點。畢竟，一則想對所有人說話的廣告，最後往往反而誰都說服不了。如
果訊息沒有打中重點，整個宣傳活動就很容易像盲目亂投一樣。]

components.english: For example, if a company is promoting sports shoes to teenagers, it may use fast-paced video, trendy music, and social media platforms. By contrast, if the target audience is working adults, the ad may need a more polished tone and more practical information.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一家公司要向青少年推廣運動鞋，它可能會使用節奏明快的影片、流行音樂，以及社交媒體平台。相比之下，如果目標受眾是上班族，廣告就可能需要更成熟的語氣，以及更多實際資訊。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a company is promoting sports shoes to teenagers, it
may use fast-paced video, trendy music, and social media platforms. By contrast,
if the target audience is working adults, the ad may need a more polished tone
and more practical information.
[例如，如果一家公司要向青少年推廣運動鞋，它可能會使用節奏明快的影片、流行音
樂，以及社交媒體平台。相比之下，如果目標受眾是上班族，廣告就可能需要更成熟的
語氣，以及更多實際資訊。]

components.english: So, to me, a good advertisement should begin with a clear understanding of the audience, because that shapes everything else.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，一則好的廣告應該先從清楚了解受眾開始，因為這會決定之後幾乎所有部分。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, a good advertisement should begin with a clear
understanding of the audience, because that shapes everything else.
[所以，在我看來，一則好的廣告應該先從清楚了解受眾開始，因為這會決定之後幾乎
所有部分。]

components.english: Another factor that should be taken into account is credibility, because even the most eye-catching advertisement can backfire if people do not trust it.
components.chinese: 另一個應該考慮的重要因素就是可信度，因為即使一則廣告再吸睛，只要人們不信任它，最後也可能適得其反。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another factor that should be taken into account is credibility,
because even the most eye-catching advertisement can backfire if people do not
trust it.
[另一個應該考慮的重要因素就是可信度，因為即使一則廣告再吸睛，只要人們不信
任它，最後也可能適得其反。]

components.english: I think advertisers need to strike a balance between creativity and honesty. Of course, an ad should be memorable and persuasive, but it should not exaggerate too much or make promises it cannot keep. Nowadays, people are exposed to advertising all the time, so they can usually see through something that feels fake or manipulative. Trust is hard to build and easy to lose, so one careless message can leave a sour taste in people’s mouths. Cultural sensitivity also matters, because an ad that is clever in one context may rub people the wrong way in another.
components.chinese: 我認為，廣告製作者需要在創意和誠信之間取得平衡。當然，廣告應該令人印象深刻，也應該有說服力，但它不應該過度誇張，或者作出根本無法兌現的承諾。現今的人每天都接觸大量廣告，所以只要某則廣告顯得虛假或帶有操控意味，他們通常很快就會看穿。信任很難建立，卻很容易失去，因此一句失當的訊息，就可能讓人留下不好的感覺。文化敏感度同樣重要，因為一則在某個環境下看似聰明的廣告，到了另一個環境，卻可能令人反感。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think advertisers need to strike a balance between creativity
and honesty. Of course, an ad should be memorable and persuasive, but it
should not exaggerate too much or make promises it cannot keep. Nowadays,
people are exposed to advertising all the time, so they can usually see through
something that feels fake or manipulative. Trust is hard to build and easy to
lose, so one careless message can leave a sour taste in people’s mouths. Cultural
sensitivity also matters, because an ad that is clever in one context may rub
people the wrong way in another.
[我認為，廣告製作者需要在創意和誠信之間取得平衡。當然，廣告應該令人印象深刻
，也應該有說服力，但它不應該過度誇張，或者作出根本無法兌現的承諾。現今的人每
天都接觸大量廣告，所以只要某則廣告顯得虛假或帶有操控意味，他們通常很快就會
看穿。信任很難建立，卻很容易失去，因此一句失當的訊息，就可能讓人留下不好的感
覺。文化敏感度同樣重要，因為一則在某個環境下看似聰明的廣告，到了另一個環境，
卻可能令人反感。]

components.english: For instance, if a beauty brand makes unrealistic claims or uses offensive stereotypes, the advertisement may go viral for all the wrong reasons. By contrast, if the message is honest, respectful, and well judged, people are far more likely to respond positively.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個美容品牌作出不切實際的宣稱，或者使用冒犯性的刻板印象，那則廣告就可能因為負面原因而瘋傳。相反，如果訊息真誠、尊重他人，而且拿捏得當，人們就更有可能作出正面反應。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a beauty brand makes unrealistic claims or uses
offensive stereotypes, the advertisement may go viral for all the wrong reasons.
By contrast, if the message is honest, respectful, and well judged, people are far
more likely to respond positively.
[例如，如果一個美容品牌作出不切實際的宣稱，或者使用冒犯性的刻板印象，那則廣
告就可能因為負面原因而瘋傳。相反，如果訊息真誠、尊重他人，而且拿捏得當，人們
就更有可能作出正面反應。]

components.english: Overall, I would say the key factors are knowing the audience, choosing the right message and medium, and making sure the advertisement is trustworthy as well as creative.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說關鍵因素包括了解受眾、選對訊息和媒介，以及確保廣告既有創意，也值得信任。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say the key factors are knowing the audience,
choosing the right message and medium, and making sure the advertisement is
trustworthy as well as creative.
[總結來說，我會說關鍵因素包括了解受眾、選對訊息和媒介，以及確保廣告既有創意
，也值得信任。]

question.english: Is advertising really necessary in modern society?
question.chinese: 在現代社會中，廣告真的有必要嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is advertising really necessary in modern society?
在現代社會中，廣告真的有必要嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think advertising is necessary in modern society, because it helps connect businesses with consumers and makes people aware of the choices available to them.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為廣告在現代社會中是有必要的，因為它能幫助企業接觸消費者，也讓人們知道自己有哪些選擇。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think advertising is necessary in modern society, because it
helps connect businesses with consumers and makes people aware of the choices
available to them.
[是的，我的確認為廣告在現代社會中是有必要的，因為它能幫助企業接觸消費者，也
讓人們知道自己有哪些選擇。]

components.english: In today’s world, people are surrounded by products, services, and information, so without advertising, it would be much harder for businesses to cut through the noise. At the same time, consumers would know far less about new products, special offers, or useful services that might genuinely improve their lives. In that sense, advertising acts like a bridge between what companies provide and what people actually need.
components.chinese: 在今天這個世界，人們每天都被產品、服務和資訊包圍，所以如果沒有廣告，企業就會更難在大量訊息之中突圍而出。與此同時，消費者也會較難知道有哪些新產品、優惠，或者真正可能改善生活的實用服務。從這個角度來看，廣告就像一座橋樑，把企業所提供的東西和人們真正需要的東西連接起來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In today’s world, people are surrounded by products, services,
and information, so without advertising, it would be much harder for
businesses to cut through the noise. At the same time, consumers would know
far less about new products, special offers, or useful services that might
genuinely improve their lives. In that sense, advertising acts like a bridge
between what companies provide and what people actually need.
[在今天這個世界，人們每天都被產品、服務和資訊包圍，所以如果沒有廣告，企業就
會更難在大量訊息之中突圍而出。與此同時，消費者也會較難知道有哪些新產品、優
惠，或者真正可能改善生活的實用服務。從這個角度來看，廣告就像一座橋樑，把企業
所提供的東西和人們真正需要的東西連接起來。]

components.english: For example, if a small local business launches a useful app or opens a new café, advertising can help people find out about it. Otherwise, even a good product may remain hidden in plain sight and never get the attention it deserves.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一間本地小企業推出一個實用的應用程式，或者開設一間新咖啡店，廣告就能幫助人們知道它的存在。否則，即使產品本身很好，也可能一直被埋沒，得不到它應有的關注。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a small local business launches a useful app or opens
a new café, advertising can help people find out about it. Otherwise, even a good
product may remain hidden in plain sight and never get the attention it
deserves.
[例如，如果一間本地小企業推出一個實用的應用程式，或者開設一間新咖啡店，廣告
就能幫助人們知道它的存在。否則，即使產品本身很好，也可能一直被埋沒，得不到它
應有的關注。]

components.english: So yes, I would say advertising is necessary, because without it, both businesses and consumers would be left somewhat in the dark.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說廣告是有必要的，因為如果沒有它，企業和消費者某程度上都會處於資訊不足的狀態。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say advertising is necessary, because without it,
both businesses and consumers would be left somewhat in the dark.
[所以，是的，我會說廣告是有必要的，因為如果沒有它，企業和消費者某程度上都會
處於資訊不足的狀態。]

components.english: That said, I do not think all advertising is good or equally necessary, because it can become excessive, manipulative, or simply overwhelming.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為所有廣告都是好的，或者同樣有必要，因為廣告也可能變得過多、帶有操控性，甚至令人喘不過氣來。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think all advertising is good or equally necessary,
because it can become excessive, manipulative, or simply overwhelming.
[不過，我並不認為所有廣告都是好的，或者同樣有必要，因為廣告也可能變得過多、
帶有操控性，甚至令人喘不過氣來。]

components.english: The real problem is not advertising itself, but the way it is sometimes used. When people are constantly bombarded with flashy, exaggerated, or emotionally manipulative messages, advertising can become a double-edged sword. Instead of informing people, it may push them to buy things they do not need or create unrealistic desires. So, while advertising plays an important role, it should be honest, responsible, and kept within reasonable limits.
components.chinese: 真正的問題其實不在於廣告本身，而在於它有時被使用的方式。當人們不停被花巧、誇張，或者帶有情緒操控意味的訊息轟炸時，廣告就會變成一把雙刃劍。它不再只是提供資訊，反而可能推動人們去買自己並不需要的東西，或者製造不切實際的慾望。所以，雖然廣告確實扮演重要角色，但它應該是真誠的、負責任的，而且要有合理分寸。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The real problem is not advertising itself, but the way it is
sometimes used. When people are constantly bombarded with flashy,
exaggerated, or emotionally manipulative messages, advertising can become a
double-edged sword. Instead of informing people, it may push them to buy
things they do not need or create unrealistic desires. So, while advertising plays
an important role, it should be honest, responsible, and kept within reasonable
limits.
[真正的問題其實不在於廣告本身，而在於它有時被使用的方式。當人們不停被花巧、
誇張，或者帶有情緒操控意味的訊息轟炸時，廣告就會變成一把雙刃劍。它不再只是
提供資訊，反而可能推動人們去買自己並不需要的東西，或者製造不切實際的慾望。
所以，雖然廣告確實扮演重要角色，但它應該是真誠的、負責任的，而且要有合理分

components.english: For instance, if children or teenagers are exposed to endless ads for junk food, beauty products, or expensive gadgets, they may start to feel pressure to want things that are not really necessary. In that case, advertising stops being helpful and starts getting under people’s skin.
components.chinese: 例如，如果小朋友或青少年不停接觸到垃圾食物、美容產品，或者昂貴電子產品的廣告，他們就可能開始對那些其實並非必要的東西產生強烈渴望。在這種情況下，廣告就不再是有幫助的，而是開始對人造成無形壓力。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if children or teenagers are exposed to endless ads
for junk food, beauty products, or expensive gadgets, they may start to feel
pressure to want things that are not really necessary. In that case, advertising
stops being helpful and starts getting under people’s skin.
[例如，如果小朋友或青少年不停接觸到垃圾食物、美容產品，或者昂貴電子產品的廣
告，他們就可能開始對那些其實並非必要的東西產生強烈渴望。在這種情況下，廣告
就不再是有幫助的，而是開始對人造成無形壓力。]

components.english: Overall, I would say advertising is necessary in modern society, but only when it is used in a fair and sensible way.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說廣告在現代社會中是有必要的，但前提是它必須以公平而合理的方式被使用。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say advertising is necessary in modern society,
but only when it is used in a fair and sensible way.
[總結來說，我會說廣告在現代社會中是有必要的，但前提是它必須以公平而合理的
方式被使用。]

question.english: How does advertising influence children?
question.chinese: 廣告如何影響孩子？

source_lines.text: Topic: How does advertising influence children?
廣告如何影響孩子？

components.english: I think advertising can influence children quite strongly, mainly by shaping what they want, what they pay attention to, and what they ask their parents to buy.
components.chinese: 我認為，廣告可以相當強烈地影響孩子，主要是透過塑造他們想要甚麼、注意甚麼，以及向父母要求購買甚麼。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think advertising can influence children quite strongly, mainly by
shaping what they want, what they pay attention to, and what they ask their
parents to buy.
[我認為，廣告可以相當強烈地影響孩子，主要是透過塑造他們想要甚麼、注意甚麼，
以及向父母要求購買甚麼。]

components.english: The main reason is that children are usually more impressionable than adults, so they tend to absorb catchy messages very easily. Bright colours, fun characters, repeated slogans, and cheerful music can all make an advertisement stick in their minds. In many cases, children soak things up like sponges, so even a simple ad can plant a seed and make a product seem exciting, desirable, or even essential. As a result, advertising often shapes not only their preferences, but also their behaviour.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，孩子通常比成年人更容易受影響，所以他們往往很容易吸收那些吸引人的訊息。鮮豔的色彩、有趣的人物、重複的口號，以及輕快的音樂，都能令一則廣告深深留在他們腦海裡。在很多情況下，孩子就像海綿一樣吸收資訊，所以即使是一則簡單的廣告，也可以在他們心裡種下一顆種子，讓某件產品看起來很吸引、很想得到，甚至像是必需品一樣。結果就是，廣告不但會影響他們的喜好，也會影響他們的行為。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that children are usually more
impressionable than adults, so they tend to absorb catchy messages very easily.
Bright colours, fun characters, repeated slogans, and cheerful music can all make
an advertisement stick in their minds. In many cases, children soak things up
like sponges, so even a simple ad can plant a seed and make a product seem
exciting, desirable, or even essential. As a result, advertising often shapes not
only their preferences, but also their behaviour.
[主要原因是，孩子通常比成年人更容易受影響，所以他們往往很容易吸收那些吸引
人的訊息。鮮豔的色彩、有趣的人物、重複的口號，以及輕快的音樂，都能令一則廣告
深深留在他們腦海裡。在很多情況下，孩子就像海綿一樣吸收資訊，所以即使是一則
簡單的廣告，也可以在他們心裡種下一顆種子，讓某件產品看起來很吸引、很想得到，
甚至像是必需品一樣。結果就是，廣告不但會影響他們的喜好，也會影響他們的行

components.english: For example, if a child keeps seeing advertisements for a toy, a sugary cereal, or a fast-food meal, they may start asking for it again and again. Even if they did not want it at first, repeated exposure can slowly wear down their resistance and make the product feel impossible to ignore.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子不斷看到玩具、含糖穀物，或者快餐套餐的廣告，他們就可能會一而再、再而三地要求購買。即使他們一開始並不特別想要，反覆接觸之下，那種抗拒也可能慢慢被磨掉，令那件產品變得幾乎無法忽視。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child keeps seeing advertisements for a toy, a
sugary cereal, or a fast-food meal, they may start asking for it again and again.
Even if they did not want it at first, repeated exposure can slowly wear down
their resistance and make the product feel impossible to ignore.
[例如，如果一個孩子不斷看到玩具、含糖穀物，或者快餐套餐的廣告，他們就可能會
一而再、再而三地要求購買。即使他們一開始並不特別想要，反覆接觸之下，那種抗拒
也可能慢慢被磨掉，令那件產品變得幾乎無法忽視。]

components.english: So, in one very direct sense, advertising influences children by shaping their wants and nudging their buying behaviour.
components.chinese: 所以，從一個很直接的角度來看，廣告會透過塑造孩子的慾望，並輕輕推動他們的購買行為，來影響他們。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in one very direct sense, advertising influences children by
shaping their wants and nudging their buying behaviour.
[所以，從一個很直接的角度來看，廣告會透過塑造孩子的慾望，並輕輕推動他們的購
買行為，來影響他們。]

components.english: Another important influence is that advertising can shape children’s values and self-image, not just their shopping habits.
components.chinese: 另一個重要的影響是，廣告不只是改變孩子的購物習慣，也可能塑造他們的價值觀和自我形象。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important influence is that advertising can shape children’s
values and self-image, not just their shopping habits.
[另一個重要的影響是，廣告不只是改變孩子的購物習慣，也可能塑造他們的價值觀
和自我形象。]

components.english: If children are constantly exposed to ads that link happiness, popularity, beauty, or success with certain products, they may start to see those things as measures of self-worth. That is where advertising can really get under their skin. It may blur the line between what they need and what they merely want, and it can also make them feel left out if they do not have the “right” clothes, gadgets, or appearance. In that sense, advertising does not just sell products; it sometimes sells a lifestyle or an identity.
components.chinese: 如果孩子不停接觸到一些把快樂、受歡迎、外表吸引力，或者成功，和某些產品連在一起的廣告，他們就可能開始把那些東西視為衡量自我價值的標準。這正是廣告真正深入影響人的地方。它可能會模糊「需要」和「想要」之間的界線，也可能令孩子因為自己沒有所謂「正確」的衣服、電子產品，或者外表，而感到格格不入。從這個角度來說，廣告不只是賣產品；有時它賣的其實是一種生活方式，甚至是一種身份認同。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If children are constantly exposed to ads that link happiness,
popularity, beauty, or success with certain products, they may start to see those
things as measures of self-worth. That is where advertising can really get under
their skin. It may blur the line between what they need and what they merely
want, and it can also make them feel left out if they do not have the “right”
clothes, gadgets, or appearance. In that sense, advertising does not just sell
products; it sometimes sells a lifestyle or an identity.
[如果孩子不停接觸到一些把快樂、受歡迎、外表吸引力，或者成功，和某些產品連在
一起的廣告，他們就可能開始把那些東西視為衡量自我價值的標準。這正是廣告真正
深入影響人的地方。它可能會模糊「需要」和「想要」之間的界線，也可能令孩子因為自
己沒有所謂「正確」的衣服、電子產品，或者外表，而感到格格不入。從這個角度來說，
廣告不只是賣產品；有時它賣的其實是一種生活方式，甚至是一種身份認同。]

components.english: For instance, if a child repeatedly sees ads showing that fashionable shoes or expensive gadgets make someone look cool and popular, they may begin to believe that fitting in depends on owning those things. That can put pressure on them and, in some cases, chip away at their confidence.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子反覆看到廣告暗示：時尚的鞋子或昂貴的電子產品會令一個人看起來更酷、更受歡迎，他們就可能開始相信，是否能融入群體，取決於自己有沒有那些東西。這樣一來，孩子便會承受壓力，而在某些情況下，這甚至會慢慢削弱他們的自信。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a child repeatedly sees ads showing that
fashionable shoes or expensive gadgets make someone look cool and popular,
they may begin to believe that fitting in depends on owning those things. That
can put pressure on them and, in some cases, chip away at their confidence.
[例如，如果一個孩子反覆看到廣告暗示：時尚的鞋子或昂貴的電子產品會令一個人看
起來更酷、更受歡迎，他們就可能開始相信，是否能融入群體，取決於自己有沒有那些
東西。這樣一來，孩子便會承受壓力，而在某些情況下，這甚至會慢慢削弱他們的自

components.english: Overall, I’d say advertising can influence children quite deeply, because it affects both what they want and how they see themselves. That said, the impact does not have to be entirely negative, provided that parents and schools help children think critically about the messages they see.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說廣告對孩子的影響可以相當深遠，因為它不但影響他們想要甚麼，也影響他們如何看待自己。不過，只要家長和學校能幫助孩子以批判思考去看待這些訊息，這種影響也未必完全是負面的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say advertising can influence children quite deeply,
because it affects both what they want and how they see themselves. That said,
the impact does not have to be entirely negative, provided that parents and
schools help children think critically about the messages they see.
[總結來說，我會說廣告對孩子的影響可以相當深遠，因為它不但影響他們想要甚麼，
也影響他們如何看待自己。不過，只要家長和學校能幫助孩子以批判思考去看待這些
訊息，這種影響也未必完全是負面的。]

question.english: Why do people like to keep pets?
question.chinese: 為甚麼人們喜歡養寵物？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do people like to keep pets?
為甚麼人們喜歡養寵物？

components.english: I think one of the main reasons people like to keep pets is that pets provide companionship and emotional comfort.
components.chinese: 我認為，人們喜歡養寵物，其中一個主要原因是寵物能提供陪伴和情感上的安慰。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the main reasons people like to keep pets is that pets
provide companionship and emotional comfort.
[我認為，人們喜歡養寵物，其中一個主要原因是寵物能提供陪伴和情感上的安慰。]

components.english: In modern life, many people feel busy, stressed, or sometimes a little lonely, so having a pet can make a home feel warmer and more alive. A pet is often a steady source of comfort, because its affection tends to be simple, genuine, and unconditional. In that sense, pets are not just animals; they often become part of the family and a real source of emotional support.
components.chinese: 在現代生活中，很多人都會感到忙碌、有壓力，甚至有時會有點孤單，所以養一隻寵物往往能讓家裡感覺更溫暖、更有生氣。寵物通常是一種穩定的安慰來源，因為牠們的感情往往很單純、真誠，而且不求回報。從這個角度來看，寵物不只是動物；牠們常常會成為家庭的一部分，也成為真正的情感支持來源。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In modern life, many people feel busy, stressed, or sometimes a
little lonely, so having a pet can make a home feel warmer and more alive. A pet
is often a steady source of comfort, because its affection tends to be simple,
genuine, and unconditional. In that sense, pets are not just animals; they often
become part of the family and a real source of emotional support.
[在現代生活中，很多人都會感到忙碌、有壓力，甚至有時會有點孤單，所以養一隻寵
物往往能讓家裡感覺更溫暖、更有生氣。寵物通常是一種穩定的安慰來源，因為牠們
的感情往往很單純、真誠，而且不求回報。從這個角度來看，寵物不只是動物；牠們常
常會成為家庭的一部分，也成為真正的情感支持來源。]

components.english: For example, after a long and exhausting day, coming home to a dog wagging its tail or a cat quietly curling up beside you can lift your mood almost at once. Moments like that may seem small, but they can mean the world to people who need comfort or company.
components.chinese: 例如，在漫長而疲累的一天過後，如果回到家時有一隻狗搖著尾巴迎接你，或者有一隻貓靜靜地蜷伏在你身旁，那種感覺幾乎可以立刻令心情好起來。這些時刻看似很小，但對於需要安慰或陪伴的人來說，往往意義非常重大。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, after a long and exhausting day, coming home to a dog
wagging its tail or a cat quietly curling up beside you can lift your mood almost
at once. Moments like that may seem small, but they can mean the world to
people who need comfort or company.
[例如，在漫長而疲累的一天過後，如果回到家時有一隻狗搖著尾巴迎接你，或者有一
隻貓靜靜地蜷伏在你身旁，那種感覺幾乎可以立刻令心情好起來。這些時刻看似很小
，但對於需要安慰或陪伴的人來說，往往意義非常重大。]

components.english: So, one big reason people keep pets is that they bring warmth, affection, and companionship into everyday life.
components.chinese: 所以，人們養寵物的一大原因，就是寵物能把溫暖、關愛和陪伴帶進日常生活之中。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one big reason people keep pets is that they bring warmth,
affection, and companionship into everyday life.
[所以，人們養寵物的一大原因，就是寵物能把溫暖、關愛和陪伴帶進日常生活之中。]

components.english: Another reason is that keeping pets can give people a sense of responsibility, routine, and purpose.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，養寵物能帶給人一種責任感、生活規律，以及目標感。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that keeping pets can give people a sense of
responsibility, routine, and purpose.
[另一個原因是，養寵物能帶給人一種責任感、生活規律，以及目標感。]

components.english: Looking after a pet means feeding it, cleaning up after it, spending time with it, and paying attention to its needs. That routine can be very grounding, especially for people whose lives feel a bit all over the place. In a way, a pet can become an anchor in daily life. On top of that, many people simply enjoy the feeling of caring for another living thing, because it gives them a sense of connection and meaning.
components.chinese: 照顧寵物意味著要餵食、清理、陪伴牠，並留意牠的需要。這種日常規律其實很能讓人踏實下來，尤其對那些生活節奏比較混亂的人來說更是如此。某程度上，寵物就像日常生活中的一個錨點。除此之外，很多人本身也很享受照顧另一個生命的感覺，因為這會帶來一種連結感和意義感。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Looking after a pet means feeding it, cleaning up after it,
spending time with it, and paying attention to its needs. That routine can be
very grounding, especially for people whose lives feel a bit all over the place. In a
way, a pet can become an anchor in daily life. On top of that, many people
simply enjoy the feeling of caring for another living thing, because it gives them
a sense of connection and meaning.
[照顧寵物意味著要餵食、清理、陪伴牠，並留意牠的需要。這種日常規律其實很能讓
人踏實下來，尤其對那些生活節奏比較混亂的人來說更是如此。某程度上，寵物就像
日常生活中的一個錨點。除此之外，很多人本身也很享受照顧另一個生命的感覺，因
為這會帶來一種連結感和意義感。]

components.english: For instance, a person who walks their dog every morning may gradually become more active and more disciplined, while a child who helps care for a rabbit or a hamster may learn patience and responsibility. So, pets can shape people’s habits as well as their emotions.
components.chinese: 例如，一個每天早上帶狗散步的人，可能會慢慢變得更活躍，也更有紀律；而一個幫忙照顧兔子或倉鼠的小朋友，則可能學會耐性和責任感。所以，寵物不但會影響人的情感，也會塑造人的生活習慣。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a person who walks their dog every morning may
gradually become more active and more disciplined, while a child who helps care
for a rabbit or a hamster may learn patience and responsibility. So, pets can
shape people’s habits as well as their emotions.
[例如，一個每天早上帶狗散步的人，可能會慢慢變得更活躍，也更有紀律；而一個幫
忙照顧兔子或倉鼠的小朋友，則可能學會耐性和責任感。所以，寵物不但會影響人的
情感，也會塑造人的生活習慣。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people like to keep pets because pets offer both emotional companionship and a sense of purpose. That said, keeping a pet is not something to take lightly, because it only works well when people are ready to give it proper time, care, and commitment.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們喜歡養寵物，是因為寵物既能提供情感上的陪伴，也能帶來生活上的目標感。不過，養寵物並不是一件可以隨便看待的事，因為只有當人們願意付出足夠的時間、照顧和承擔時，這件事才會真正變得美好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people like to keep pets because pets offer both
emotional companionship and a sense of purpose. That said, keeping a pet is not
something to take lightly, because it only works well when people are ready to
give it proper time, care, and commitment.
[總結來說，我會說人們喜歡養寵物，是因為寵物既能提供情感上的陪伴，也能帶來生
活上的目標感。不過，養寵物並不是一件可以隨便看待的事，因為只有當人們願意付
出足夠的時間、照顧和承擔時，這件事才會真正變得美好。]

question.english: What should we do to protect endangered animals?
question.chinese: 我們應該怎樣做去保護瀕危動物？

source_lines.text: Topic: What should we do to protect endangered animals?
我們應該怎樣做去保護瀕危動物？

components.english: I think the first thing we should do is protect animals’ natural habitats and enforce stronger laws against hunting and illegal trade.
components.chinese: 我認為，我們首先應該做的，就是保護動物的自然棲息地，並且更嚴格地執行打擊捕獵和非法買賣的法律。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the first thing we should do is protect animals’ natural habitats
and enforce stronger laws against hunting and illegal trade.
[我認為，我們首先應該做的，就是保護動物的自然棲息地，並且更嚴格地執行打擊捕
獵和非法買賣的法律。]

components.english: The main reason is that many endangered animals are not disappearing for no reason; they are being pushed to the edge by habitat destruction, pollution, and human activity. If forests are cut down and ecosystems are torn apart, animals simply have nowhere to go. In other words, if we do not protect the places where they live, we are fighting a losing battle. Stronger laws also matter, because without real consequences, poaching and wildlife trafficking will continue behind the scenes.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，很多瀕危動物並不是無緣無故消失的；牠們其實是被棲息地破壞、污染，以及各種人類活動一步一步逼到絕境。如果森林被砍伐，整個生態系統被破壞，動物根本就無處可去。換句話說，如果我們連牠們生活的地方都保不住，那麼這場保育工作很可能從一開始就注定處於下風。更嚴格的法律同樣重要，因為如果沒有真正的代價，偷獵和非法野生動物買賣就仍然會在暗地裡持續發生。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that many endangered animals are not
disappearing for no reason; they are being pushed to the edge by habitat
destruction, pollution, and human activity. If forests are cut down and
ecosystems are torn apart, animals simply have nowhere to go. In other words, if
we do not protect the places where they live, we are fighting a losing battle.
Stronger laws also matter, because without real consequences, poaching and
wildlife trafficking will continue behind the scenes.
[主要原因是，很多瀕危動物並不是無緣無故消失的；牠們其實是被棲息地破壞、污染
，以及各種人類活動一步一步逼到絕境。如果森林被砍伐，整個生態系統被破壞，動物
根本就無處可去。換句話說，如果我們連牠們生活的地方都保不住，那麼這場保育工
作很可能從一開始就注定處於下風。更嚴格的法律同樣重要，因為如果沒有真正的代
價，偷獵和非法野生動物買賣就仍然會在暗地裡持續發生。]

components.english: For example, governments can create more protected areas, limit deforestation, and impose heavier penalties on people who hunt rare animals or sell products made from them. Once the habitat is preserved and the law has real teeth, endangered species stand a much better chance of survival.
components.chinese: 例如，政府可以設立更多受保護區、限制砍伐森林，並且對捕獵珍稀動物或販賣相關產品的人施加更重的懲罰。當棲息地得到保護，而法律又真正有震懾力時，瀕危物種的生存機會自然會大大提高。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, governments can create more protected areas, limit
deforestation, and impose heavier penalties on people who hunt rare animals or
sell products made from them. Once the habitat is preserved and the law has
real teeth, endangered species stand a much better chance of survival.
[例如，政府可以設立更多受保護區、限制砍伐森林，並且對捕獵珍稀動物或販賣相關
產品的人施加更重的懲罰。當棲息地得到保護，而法律又真正有震懾力時，瀕危物種
的生存機會自然會大大提高。]

components.english: So, to me, protecting habitats and cracking down on illegal activities should be the top priority.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，保護棲息地，以及嚴厲打擊非法活動，應該是首要任務。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, protecting habitats and cracking down on illegal
activities should be the top priority.
[所以，在我看來，保護棲息地，以及嚴厲打擊非法活動，應該是首要任務。]

components.english: On top of that, I think education and public awareness are just as important, because long-term protection depends on changing human behaviour.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為教育和公眾意識同樣重要，因為長遠的保護始終取決於人類行為是否改變。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think education and public awareness are just as
important, because long-term protection depends on changing human
behaviour.
[除此之外，我也認為教育和公眾意識同樣重要，因為長遠的保護始終取決於人類行
為是否改變。]

components.english: Laws can help, but they cannot do everything on their own. If people keep buying products made from endangered animals, wasting natural resources, or treating wildlife as a source of profit, the problem will keep coming back. That is why schools, the media, and even social campaigns have a role to play. The more people understand that every species is a thread in the web of life, the more likely they are to act responsibly.
components.chinese: 法律固然有幫助，但單靠法律並不能解決所有問題。如果人們仍然繼續購買由瀕危動物製成的產品、浪費自然資源，或者把野生動物當成賺錢工具，那麼問題始終會一再出現。這就是為甚麼學校、媒體，甚至社會宣傳都扮演著重要角色。人們越明白每一個物種都是生命之網中的一條線，他們就越有可能作出負責任的行動。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Laws can help, but they cannot do everything on their own. If
people keep buying products made from endangered animals, wasting natural
resources, or treating wildlife as a source of profit, the problem will keep coming
back. That is why schools, the media, and even social campaigns have a role to
play. The more people understand that every species is a thread in the web of
life, the more likely they are to act responsibly.
[法律固然有幫助，但單靠法律並不能解決所有問題。如果人們仍然繼續購買由瀕危
動物製成的產品、浪費自然資源，或者把野生動物當成賺錢工具，那麼問題始終會一
再出現。這就是為甚麼學校、媒體，甚至社會宣傳都扮演著重要角色。人們越明白每一
個物種都是生命之網中的一條線，他們就越有可能作出負責任的行動。]

components.english: For instance, children can be taught from a young age to respect nature, while adults can support wildlife charities, avoid buying animal-based luxury goods, and choose more sustainable lifestyles. Bit by bit, those choices can add up, because conservation is not only the government’s job; it is everyone’s responsibility.
components.chinese: 例如，孩子可以從小被教導去尊重自然，而成年人則可以支持野生動物保育機構、拒絕購買以動物製成的奢侈品，並且選擇更可持續的生活方式。這些選擇看似微小，但慢慢累積起來卻能產生影響，因為保育不只是政府的責任，而是每一個人的責任。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, children can be taught from a young age to respect
nature, while adults can support wildlife charities, avoid buying animal-based
luxury goods, and choose more sustainable lifestyles. Bit by bit, those choices
can add up, because conservation is not only the government’s job; it is
everyone’s responsibility.
[例如，孩子可以從小被教導去尊重自然，而成年人則可以支持野生動物保育機構、拒
絕購買以動物製成的奢侈品，並且選擇更可持續的生活方式。這些選擇看似微小，但
慢慢累積起來卻能產生影響，因為保育不只是政府的責任，而是每一個人的責任。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say we need both strong protection measures and a change in public attitude. If we only care when it is too late, some species may disappear for good.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我們既需要強而有力的保護措施，也需要改變公眾的態度。如果我們總是等到太遲才開始在乎，那麼有些物種可能就會永遠消失。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say we need both strong protection measures and a
change in public attitude. If we only care when it is too late, some species may
disappear for good.
[總結來說，我會說我們既需要強而有力的保護措施，也需要改變公眾的態度。如果我
們總是等到太遲才開始在乎，那麼有些物種可能就會永遠消失。]

question.english: Do you support doing experiments on animals?
question.chinese: 你支持在動物身上進行實驗嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you support doing experiments on animals?
你支持在動物身上進行實驗嗎？

components.english: Personally, I only support animal experiments in very limited situations, mainly when they are necessary for serious medical research and there is no realistic alternative.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我只會在非常有限的情況下支持動物實驗，主要是當它對嚴肅的醫學研究確實有必要，而且沒有現實可行的替代方法時。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I only support animal experiments in very limited situations,
mainly when they are necessary for serious medical research and there is no
realistic alternative.
[就我個人而言，我只會在非常有限的情況下支持動物實驗，主要是當它對嚴肅的醫
學研究確實有必要，而且沒有現實可行的替代方法時。]

components.english: The reason is that animal testing raises a real ethical dilemma. On the one hand, I do not think it is right to cause suffering to animals without a very strong reason. On the other hand, if a piece of research could save human lives or lead to a treatment for a serious disease, it may be seen as a necessary evil. That said, it should only be used as a last resort, under strict regulation, and with every effort made to reduce harm. Otherwise, it is a slippery slope.
components.chinese: 原因是，動物實驗確實牽涉到一個真實而複雜的道德兩難。一方面，我並不認為在沒有充分理由的情況下令動物受苦是正確的。另一方面，如果某項研究有機會拯救人命，或者為嚴重疾病帶來治療方法，那麼它在某些情況下可能會被視為一種不得已的做法。不過，這種做法只應該被當成最後手段，而且必須受到嚴格監管，並盡一切努力把傷害減到最低。否則，事情很容易一步一步失控。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that animal testing raises a real ethical dilemma.
On the one hand, I do not think it is right to cause suffering to animals without
a very strong reason. On the other hand, if a piece of research could save human
lives or lead to a treatment for a serious disease, it may be seen as a necessary
evil. That said, it should only be used as a last resort, under strict regulation,
and with every effort made to reduce harm. Otherwise, it is a slippery slope.
[原因是，動物實驗確實牽涉到一個真實而複雜的道德兩難。一方面，我並不認為在沒
有充分理由的情況下令動物受苦是正確的。另一方面，如果某項研究有機會拯救人命
，或者為嚴重疾病帶來治療方法，那麼它在某些情況下可能會被視為一種不得已的做
法。不過，這種做法只應該被當成最後手段，而且必須受到嚴格監管，並盡一切努力把
傷害減到最低。否則，事情很容易一步一步失控。]

components.english: For example, if scientists are developing a treatment for a life-threatening illness and computer models or lab tests cannot provide enough reliable information, I can understand why limited animal testing might still be used. In that case, the goal is not profit or convenience, but the possibility of saving lives.
components.chinese: 例如，如果科學家正在研發一種針對致命疾病的治療方法，而電腦模擬或實驗室測試都無法提供足夠可靠的資料，那麼我可以理解為甚麼有限度的動物實驗仍然可能被使用。在這種情況下，目的並不是為了賺錢或圖方便，而是為了拯救生命的可能性。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if scientists are developing a treatment for a
life-threatening illness and computer models or lab tests cannot provide enough
reliable information, I can understand why limited animal testing might still be
used. In that case, the goal is not profit or convenience, but the possibility of
saving lives.
[例如，如果科學家正在研發一種針對致命疾病的治療方法，而電腦模擬或實驗室測
試都無法提供足夠可靠的資料，那麼我可以理解為甚麼有限度的動物實驗仍然可能被
使用。在這種情況下，目的並不是為了賺錢或圖方便，而是為了拯救生命的可能性。]

components.english: So, I would say I support it only conditionally, and only when the purpose is truly important and the standards are extremely strict.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說我只是在有條件的情況下支持，而且前提是研究目的真的非常重要，並且標準必須極為嚴格。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I would say I support it only conditionally, and only when
the purpose is truly important and the standards are extremely strict.
[所以，我會說我只是在有條件的情況下支持，而且前提是研究目的真的非常重要，並
且標準必須極為嚴格。]

components.english: However, I do not support animal testing for things like cosmetics or other non-essential consumer products.
components.chinese: 不過，對於化妝品或其他並非必要的消費產品，我並不支持使用動物實驗。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: However, I do not support animal testing for things like cosmetics or
other non-essential consumer products.
[不過，對於化妝品或其他並非必要的消費產品，我並不支持使用動物實驗。]

components.english: That is where I would draw the line. If the purpose is simply to make a product more marketable or to give a company a commercial edge, then the moral cost is far too high. To be honest, it is hard to stomach the idea of animals suffering just so people can buy a new lipstick or a better shampoo. In cases like that, I think society should push much harder for alternative testing methods instead of taking the easy way out.
components.chinese: 那就是我會劃清界線的地方。如果目的是單純令產品更容易銷售，或者讓公司在商業競爭上佔優勢，那麼那種道德代價實在太高了。老實說，只是為了讓人買一支新的唇膏，或者一款更好的洗髮水，就要動物受苦，這種想法本身就令人難以接受。在這類情況下，我認為社會應該更積極推動替代測試方法，而不是走最省事的路。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: That is where I would draw the line. If the purpose is simply
to make a product more marketable or to give a company a commercial edge,
then the moral cost is far too high. To be honest, it is hard to stomach the idea
of animals suffering just so people can buy a new lipstick or a better shampoo.
In cases like that, I think society should push much harder for alternative
testing methods instead of taking the easy way out.
[那就是我會劃清界線的地方。如果目的是單純令產品更容易銷售，或者讓公司在商
業競爭上佔優勢，那麼那種道德代價實在太高了。老實說，只是為了讓人買一支新的
唇膏，或者一款更好的洗髮水，就要動物受苦，這種想法本身就令人難以接受。在這類
情況下，我認為社會應該更積極推動替代測試方法，而不是走最省事的路。]

components.english: For instance, if a beauty brand tests a new makeup product on animals just to improve sales, I do not think that can really be justified. It seems completely out of proportion to the benefit involved.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個美容品牌為了提升銷量，而在動物身上測試新的化妝產品，我並不認為這種做法真的站得住腳。因為它所付出的代價，和最後得到的好處，明顯不成比例。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a beauty brand tests a new makeup product on
animals just to improve sales, I do not think that can really be justified. It seems
completely out of proportion to the benefit involved.
[例如，如果一個美容品牌為了提升銷量，而在動物身上測試新的化妝產品，我並不認
為這種做法真的站得住腳。因為它所付出的代價，和最後得到的好處，明顯不成比

components.english: Overall, I take a middle position: I can accept animal experiments as a last resort in vital medical research, but beyond that, I do not support them.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的立場算是比較中間的：如果是在關乎重大的醫學研究中，並且已經到了最後手段，我可以勉強接受動物實驗；但除此之外，我並不支持。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I take a middle position: I can accept animal
experiments as a last resort in vital medical research, but beyond that, I do not
support them.
[總結來說，我的立場算是比較中間的：如果是在關乎重大的醫學研究中，並且已經到
了最後手段，我可以勉強接受動物實驗；但除此之外，我並不支持。]

question.english: Why do some people refuse to eat animals?
question.chinese: 為甚麼有些人拒絕吃動物？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do some people refuse to eat animals?
為甚麼有些人拒絕吃動物？

components.english: I think one of the main reasons is ethical concern, because some people simply believe it is wrong to kill animals for food.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個主要原因是道德考量，因為有些人就是單純相信，為了食物而殺害動物是不對的。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the main reasons is ethical concern, because some people
simply believe it is wrong to kill animals for food.
[我認為，其中一個主要原因是道德考量，因為有些人就是單純相信，為了食物而殺害
動物是不對的。]

components.english: For some people, animals are not just products on a plate; they are living creatures that can feel pain, fear, and distress. Once people start to see animals that way, eating them may no longer sit right with them. In other words, it becomes a matter of conscience rather than taste. They may feel that if human beings can survive without eating meat, then causing suffering for the sake of habit or convenience is hard to justify.
components.chinese: 對某些人來說，動物不只是盤中的食物，而是會感受痛苦、恐懼和壓力的生命。一旦人開始用這種角度看待動物，吃牠們這件事就可能不再讓人心安。換句話說，這已經不再只是口味問題，而是良心上的選擇。他們可能會覺得，如果人類其實可以不靠吃肉也能生活，那麼為了習慣或方便而令動物受苦，就很難說得過去。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: For some people, animals are not just products on a plate; they
are living creatures that can feel pain, fear, and distress. Once people start to see
animals that way, eating them may no longer sit right with them. In other
words, it becomes a matter of conscience rather than taste. They may feel that if
human beings can survive without eating meat, then causing suffering for the
sake of habit or convenience is hard to justify.
[對某些人來說，動物不只是盤中的食物，而是會感受痛苦、恐懼和壓力的生命。一旦
人開始用這種角度看待動物，吃牠們這件事就可能不再讓人心安。換句話說，這已經
不再只是口味問題，而是良心上的選擇。他們可能會覺得，如果人類其實可以不靠吃
肉也能生活，那麼為了習慣或方便而令動物受苦，就很難說得過去。]

components.english: For example, someone who loves animals and keeps pets may find it emotionally difficult to draw a sharp line between animals they care for and animals they eat. To that person, refusing to eat meat may feel like the only choice that truly matches their values.
components.chinese: 例如，一個很喜歡動物、甚至自己也有養寵物的人，可能會覺得很難在自己照顧的動物和自己吃的動物之間劃出一道很明確的界線。對這種人來說，不吃肉可能就是唯一真正符合自己價值觀的選擇。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone who loves animals and keeps pets may find it
emotionally difficult to draw a sharp line between animals they care for and
animals they eat. To that person, refusing to eat meat may feel like the only
choice that truly matches their values.
[例如，一個很喜歡動物、甚至自己也有養寵物的人，可能會覺得很難在自己照顧的動
物和自己吃的動物之間劃出一道很明確的界線。對這種人來說，不吃肉可能就是唯一
真正符合自己價值觀的選擇。]

components.english: So, one major reason people refuse to eat animals is that they see it as a moral issue rather than a dietary preference.
components.chinese: 所以，人們拒絕吃動物的一個重要原因，就是他們把這件事看成道德問題，而不只是飲食偏好。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major reason people refuse to eat animals is that they see
it as a moral issue rather than a dietary preference.
[所以，人們拒絕吃動物的一個重要原因，就是他們把這件事看成道德問題，而不只是
飲食偏好。]

components.english: Another reason is that some people are concerned about health or the environment, so they choose to avoid animal products altogether.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，有些人關心健康或環境，因此選擇完全避免食用動物製品。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that some people are concerned about health or the
environment, so they choose to avoid animal products altogether.
[另一個原因是，有些人關心健康或環境，因此選擇完全避免食用動物製品。]

components.english: From a health point of view, some people feel that a plant-based diet is lighter, cleaner, or better for their bodies. From an environmental point of view, others believe that large-scale meat production puts too much pressure on land, water, and the climate. So, for them, refusing to eat animals is not only about what is on the plate; it is also about the bigger picture. In that sense, food becomes a reflection of personal values and lifestyle choices.
components.chinese: 從健康角度來看，有些人覺得以植物為主的飲食對身體來說更清淡、更乾淨，或者更有益。從環境角度來看，另一些人則相信，大規模肉類生產對土地、水資源和氣候造成了太大壓力。所以，對他們而言，拒絕吃動物不只是關於盤子裡有甚麼，而是關於更大的整體問題。從這個角度來說，食物就成了個人價值觀和生活方式的一種反映。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: From a health point of view, some people feel that a
plant-based diet is lighter, cleaner, or better for their bodies. From an
environmental point of view, others believe that large-scale meat production
puts too much pressure on land, water, and the climate. So, for them, refusing to
eat animals is not only about what is on the plate; it is also about the bigger
picture. In that sense, food becomes a reflection of personal values and lifestyle
choices.
[從健康角度來看，有些人覺得以植物為主的飲食對身體來說更清淡、更乾淨，或者更
有益。從環境角度來看，另一些人則相信，大規模肉類生產對土地、水資源和氣候造成
了太大壓力。所以，對他們而言，拒絕吃動物不只是關於盤子裡有甚麼，而是關於更大
的整體問題。從這個角度來說，食物就成了個人價值觀和生活方式的一種反映。]

components.english: For instance, a person may decide to stop eating meat after learning more about factory farming or after noticing that vegetarian meals make them feel healthier and more energetic. Little by little, that choice can become second nature rather than a sacrifice.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能在更了解工廠式養殖之後，決定不再吃肉；又或者他們發現素食令自己感覺更健康、更有精神，於是改變飲食習慣。久而久之，這種選擇就會變得很自然，而不再像是一種犧牲。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a person may decide to stop eating meat after
learning more about factory farming or after noticing that vegetarian meals
make them feel healthier and more energetic. Little by little, that choice can
become second nature rather than a sacrifice.
[例如，一個人可能在更了解工廠式養殖之後，決定不再吃肉；又或者他們發現素食令
自己感覺更健康、更有精神，於是改變飲食習慣。久而久之，這種選擇就會變得很自然
，而不再像是一種犧牲。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people refuse to eat animals for a mixture of ethical, environmental, and health-related reasons.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，人們拒絕吃動物，通常是出於道德、環境，以及健康等多方面原因的結合。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people refuse to eat animals for a mixture of
ethical, environmental, and health-related reasons.
[總結來說，我會說，人們拒絕吃動物，通常是出於道德、環境，以及健康等多方面原
因的結合。]

question.english: What would happen when some species disappear on earth?
question.chinese: 如果地球上有些物種消失了，會發生甚麼事？

source_lines.text: Topic: What would happen when some species disappear on earth?
如果地球上有些物種消失了，會發生甚麼事？

components.english: I think the disappearance of some species can seriously damage the natural world, because every species plays a part in keeping ecosystems in balance.
components.chinese: 我認為，有些物種一旦消失，會對自然世界造成相當嚴重的破壞，因為每一個物種都在維持生態平衡中扮演一定角色。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the disappearance of some species can seriously damage the
natural world, because every species plays a part in keeping ecosystems in
balance.
[我認為，有些物種一旦消失，會對自然世界造成相當嚴重的破壞，因為每一個物種都
在維持生態平衡中扮演一定角色。]

components.english: Nature is a bit like a web, in the sense that different species are connected to one another in ways people do not always notice at first. If one species disappears, it can upset the food chain, affect reproduction, and weaken the stability of the whole ecosystem. What seems like the loss of just one animal or plant can actually trigger a domino effect. Once one piece is removed, others may start to fall as well.
components.chinese: 大自然有點像一張網，因為不同物種之間其實彼此相連，只是人們未必一開始就看得出來。如果其中一個物種消失了，就可能打亂食物鏈、影響繁殖，並削弱整個生態系統的穩定性。表面上看起來好像只是失去了一種動物或植物，但實際上卻可能引發骨牌效應。一旦其中一塊倒下，其他部分也可能陸續受到影響。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Nature is a bit like a web, in the sense that different species are
connected to one another in ways people do not always notice at first. If one
species disappears, it can upset the food chain, affect reproduction, and weaken
the stability of the whole ecosystem. What seems like the loss of just one animal
or plant can actually trigger a domino effect. Once one piece is removed, others
may start to fall as well.
[大自然有點像一張網，因為不同物種之間其實彼此相連，只是人們未必一開始就看
得出來。如果其中一個物種消失了，就可能打亂食物鏈、影響繁殖，並削弱整個生態系
統的穩定性。表面上看起來好像只是失去了一種動物或植物，但實際上卻可能引發骨
牌效應。一旦其中一塊倒下，其他部分也可能陸續受到影響。]

components.english: For example, if bees disappeared, many plants and crops would no longer be pollinated properly. That would affect not only flowers and fruits, but also the animals and people who depend on them for food. In that kind of situation, the impact would spread far beyond the loss of a single species.
components.chinese: 例如，如果蜜蜂消失了，很多植物和農作物就無法正常授粉。這不但會影響花朵和水果，也會影響那些依賴它們作為食物來源的動物和人類。在這種情況下，影響就絕對不只是失去單一物種那麼簡單。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if bees disappeared, many plants and crops would no
longer be pollinated properly. That would affect not only flowers and fruits, but
also the animals and people who depend on them for food. In that kind of
situation, the impact would spread far beyond the loss of a single species.
[例如，如果蜜蜂消失了，很多植物和農作物就無法正常授粉。這不但會影響花朵和水
果，也會影響那些依賴它們作為食物來源的動物和人類。在這種情況下，影響就絕對
不只是失去單一物種那麼簡單。]

components.english: So, one major consequence of species loss is that it can throw entire ecosystems off balance and create problems that spread much further than people expect.
components.chinese: 所以，物種消失的一個主要後果，就是它可能令整個生態系統失去平衡，並引發比人們想像中更廣泛的問題。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major consequence of species loss is that it can throw
entire ecosystems off balance and create problems that spread much further than
people expect.
[所以，物種消失的一個主要後果，就是它可能令整個生態系統失去平衡，並引發比人
們想像中更廣泛的問題。]

components.english: Another important point is that when species disappear, human life is affected as well, not just wildlife.
components.chinese: 另一個重要的重點是，當物種消失時，受到影響的不只是野生動物，人類生活本身也會受到波及。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important point is that when species disappear, human life is
affected as well, not just wildlife.
[另一個重要的重點是，當物種消失時，受到影響的不只是野生動物，人類生活本身也
會受到波及。]

components.english: Human beings rely on nature for food, medicine, clean water, and economic activities such as farming and fishing. If species vanish, we do not just lose part of nature; we also lose resources, resilience, and future possibilities. In a way, biodiversity is like a safety net. The richer and stronger it is, the better the planet can cope with pressure. If that net is torn apart, both nature and human society become more fragile.
components.chinese: 人類依賴自然來獲取食物、藥物、潔淨水源，以及農業和漁業等經濟活動。如果物種消失，我們失去的不只是大自然的一部分，同時也會失去資源、韌性，以及未來的可能性。某程度上，生物多樣性就像一張安全網。它越豐富、越穩固，地球就越能承受壓力。如果這張網被撕裂，不但自然界會變得脆弱，人類社會也會一樣。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Human beings rely on nature for food, medicine, clean water,
and economic activities such as farming and fishing. If species vanish, we do not
just lose part of nature; we also lose resources, resilience, and future possibilities.
In a way, biodiversity is like a safety net. The richer and stronger it is, the better
the planet can cope with pressure. If that net is torn apart, both nature and
human society become more fragile.
[人類依賴自然來獲取食物、藥物、潔淨水源，以及農業和漁業等經濟活動。如果物種
消失，我們失去的不只是大自然的一部分，同時也會失去資源、韌性，以及未來的可能
性。某程度上，生物多樣性就像一張安全網。它越豐富、越穩固，地球就越能承受壓
力。如果這張網被撕裂，不但自然界會變得脆弱，人類社會也會一樣。]

components.english: For instance, if certain fish species disappeared, some coastal communities could lose both food and income. In the same way, if rare plants became extinct before being studied properly, potential medical discoveries might be lost forever. That would be a real tragedy, because once a species is gone, there is usually no way to bring it back.
components.chinese: 例如，如果某些魚類消失了，一些沿海社區就可能同時失去食物來源和收入。同樣地，如果一些珍稀植物在還未被充分研究之前就已經絕種，那些潛在的醫學發現也可能永遠消失。那會是一種真正的悲劇，因為一個物種一旦消失，通常就再也無法挽回。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if certain fish species disappeared, some coastal
communities could lose both food and income. In the same way, if rare plants
became extinct before being studied properly, potential medical discoveries
might be lost forever. That would be a real tragedy, because once a species is
gone, there is usually no way to bring it back.
[例如，如果某些魚類消失了，一些沿海社區就可能同時失去食物來源和收入。同樣地
，如果一些珍稀植物在還未被充分研究之前就已經絕種，那些潛在的醫學發現也可能
永遠消失。那會是一種真正的悲劇，因為一個物種一旦消失，通常就再也無法挽回。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the disappearance of species would have both environmental and human consequences. It would weaken ecosystems, threaten livelihoods, and make the planet poorer in every sense of the word.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說物種消失會同時帶來生態和人類層面的後果。它不但會削弱生態系統、威脅人們的生計，也會在各種意義上令這個地球變得更加貧乏。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the disappearance of species would have both
environmental and human consequences. It would weaken ecosystems, threaten
livelihoods, and make the planet poorer in every sense of the word.
[總結來說，我會說物種消失會同時帶來生態和人類層面的後果。它不但會削弱生態
系統、威脅人們的生計，也會在各種意義上令這個地球變得更加貧乏。]

question.english: Do you think that aquatic animals are dangerous?
question.chinese: 你認為水生動物危險嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think that aquatic animals are dangerous?
你認為水生動物危險嗎？

components.english: Personally, I think some aquatic animals can be dangerous, but it would be unfair to paint them all with the same brush.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為有些水生動物的確可能有危險性，但如果把所有水生動物一概而論，那就不太公平了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think some aquatic animals can be dangerous, but it would
be unfair to paint them all with the same brush.
[就我個人而言，我認為有些水生動物的確可能有危險性，但如果把所有水生動物一
概而論，那就不太公平了。]

components.english: Certain animals, such as sharks, jellyfish, stingrays, or crocodiles, can clearly cause serious harm under the wrong circumstances. However, most aquatic animals are not naturally out to get human beings. In many cases, they only become dangerous when they feel threatened, are protecting themselves, or are disturbed in their own habitat. In other words, the danger often depends as much on the situation as on the animal itself.
components.chinese: 某些動物，例如鯊魚、水母、魟魚，或者鱷魚，在不適當的情況下，確實可能造成嚴重傷害。不過，大多數水生動物其實並不是天生就想攻擊人類。在很多情況下，牠們之所以變得危險，只是因為牠們感到受威脅、正在自我保護，或者在人類打擾了牠們原本的棲息地時作出反應。換句話說，危險往往不只是來自動物本身，也和當時的情境有很大關係。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Certain animals, such as sharks, jellyfish, stingrays, or
crocodiles, can clearly cause serious harm under the wrong circumstances.
However, most aquatic animals are not naturally out to get human beings. In
many cases, they only become dangerous when they feel threatened, are
protecting themselves, or are disturbed in their own habitat. In other words,
the danger often depends as much on the situation as on the animal itself.
[某些動物，例如鯊魚、水母、魟魚，或者鱷魚，在不適當的情況下，確實可能造成嚴重
傷害。不過，大多數水生動物其實並不是天生就想攻擊人類。在很多情況下，牠們之所
以變得危險，只是因為牠們感到受威脅、正在自我保護，或者在人類打擾了牠們原本
的棲息地時作出反應。換句話說，危險往往不只是來自動物本身，也和當時的情境有
很大關係。]

components.english: For example, if someone swims in an area where there are strong warnings about jellyfish or enters waters known for sharks without proper caution, the risk obviously goes up. In that kind of situation, it is not just a question of dangerous animals; it is also a question of human judgment.
components.chinese: 例如，如果有人在明明有水母警告的地方游泳，或者在已知有鯊魚出沒的水域裡沒有做好防備就下水，那麼風險自然會大大提高。在這種情況下，問題不只是動物危不危險，同時也是人類判斷是否得當的問題。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone swims in an area where there are strong
warnings about jellyfish or enters waters known for sharks without proper
caution, the risk obviously goes up. In that kind of situation, it is not just a
question of dangerous animals; it is also a question of human judgment.
[例如，如果有人在明明有水母警告的地方游泳，或者在已知有鯊魚出沒的水域裡沒
有做好防備就下水，那麼風險自然會大大提高。在這種情況下，問題不只是動物危不
危險，同時也是人類判斷是否得當的問題。]

components.english: So, I would say some aquatic animals are dangerous, but only a relatively small number of them pose a real threat to people.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說，某些水生動物的確是危險的，但真正對人類構成明顯威脅的，其實只是相對少數。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I would say some aquatic animals are dangerous, but only a
relatively small number of them pose a real threat to people.
[所以，我會說，某些水生動物的確是危險的，但真正對人類構成明顯威脅的，其實只
是相對少數。]

components.english: At the same time, I think people often exaggerate the danger of aquatic animals, mainly because fear spreads faster than facts.
components.chinese: 與此同時，我也認為，人們往往會誇大水生動物的危險性，主要是因為恐懼傳播得往往比事實更快。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: At the same time, I think people often exaggerate the danger of
aquatic animals, mainly because fear spreads faster than facts.
[與此同時，我也認為，人們往往會誇大水生動物的危險性，主要是因為恐懼傳播得往
往比事實更快。]

components.english: Films, news reports, and dramatic stories tend to make these animals seem more frightening than they really are. As a result, people may see the ocean as a place full of threats, when in reality most sea creatures simply want to be left alone. To be honest, human beings are often more dangerous to aquatic animals than the other way round. So, rather than living in fear, I think people should learn how to respect wildlife and behave sensibly around it.
components.chinese: 電影、新聞報道，以及一些戲劇化的故事，往往會把這些動物塑造成比現實更可怕的形象。結果就是，人們可能會把海洋看成一個充滿威脅的地方，但事實上，大多數海洋生物其實只是想安安靜靜地生活。老實說，很多時候，人類對水生動物造成的危害，反而比牠們對人類造成的更大。所以，我認為與其活在恐懼之中，人們不如學會如何尊重野生動物，並且以理智的方式和牠們相處。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Films, news reports, and dramatic stories tend to make these
animals seem more frightening than they really are. As a result, people may see
the ocean as a place full of threats, when in reality most sea creatures simply
want to be left alone. To be honest, human beings are often more dangerous to
aquatic animals than the other way round. So, rather than living in fear, I think
people should learn how to respect wildlife and behave sensibly around it.
[電影、新聞報道，以及一些戲劇化的故事，往往會把這些動物塑造成比現實更可怕的
形象。結果就是，人們可能會把海洋看成一個充滿威脅的地方，但事實上，大多數海洋
生物其實只是想安安靜靜地生活。老實說，很多時候，人類對水生動物造成的危害，反
而比牠們對人類造成的更大。所以，我認為與其活在恐懼之中，人們不如學會如何尊
重野生動物，並且以理智的方式和牠們相處。]

components.english: For instance, dolphins, turtles, and most fish are not creatures people should panic about. If swimmers, divers, or tourists keep a respectful distance and follow safety rules, there is usually no reason for trouble. That way, people can enjoy the beauty of aquatic life without making a mountain out of a molehill.
components.chinese: 例如，海豚、海龜，以及大多數魚類，其實都不是人們需要過分害怕的生物。只要游泳者、潛水者，或者遊客保持適當距離，並遵守安全規則，通常就沒有甚麼理由會出問題。這樣一來，人們就可以欣賞水生生物的美，而不必把事情小題大做。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, dolphins, turtles, and most fish are not creatures
people should panic about. If swimmers, divers, or tourists keep a respectful
distance and follow safety rules, there is usually no reason for trouble. That way,
people can enjoy the beauty of aquatic life without making a mountain out of a
molehill.
[例如，海豚、海龜，以及大多數魚類，其實都不是人們需要過分害怕的生物。只要游
泳者、潛水者，或者遊客保持適當距離，並遵守安全規則，通常就沒有甚麼理由會出問
題。這樣一來，人們就可以欣賞水生生物的美，而不必把事情小題大做。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say aquatic animals can be dangerous, but the risk is often overstated. What really matters is understanding which animals are risky and knowing how to behave responsibly around them.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說水生動物確實可能有危險性，但那種風險往往被誇大了。真正重要的是了解哪些動物較有風險，並且知道應該如何以負責任的方式和牠們相處。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say aquatic animals can be dangerous, but the risk
is often overstated. What really matters is understanding which animals are
risky and knowing how to behave responsibly around them.
[總結來說，我會說水生動物確實可能有危險性，但那種風險往往被誇大了。真正重要
的是了解哪些動物較有風險，並且知道應該如何以負責任的方式和牠們相處。]

question.english: A lot of animals are getting extinct these days. What do you think about this? Why is it happening?
question.chinese: 近年有很多動物正在絕種。你對這件事有甚麼看法？為甚麼會發生？

source_lines.text: Topic: A lot of animals are getting extinct these days.
What do you think about this? Why is it happening?
近年有很多動物正在絕種。你對這件事有甚麼看法？為甚麼會發生？

components.english: I think it is deeply worrying, because the extinction of animals is not just sad in itself; it is also a warning sign that the natural world is being pushed too far.
components.chinese: 我認為這是非常令人憂慮的，因為動物絕種不只是本身令人難過；它同時也是一個警號，顯示自然世界正被逼到過度承受的地步。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think it is deeply worrying, because the extinction of animals is not
just sad in itself; it is also a warning sign that the natural world is being pushed
too far.
[我認為這是非常令人憂慮的，因為動物絕種不只是本身令人難過；它同時也是一個警
號，顯示自然世界正被逼到過度承受的地步。]

components.english: Every time a species disappears, the planet becomes a little poorer. In my view, each species is like a thread in the web of life, so when one is removed, the whole system becomes weaker and more fragile. What makes it even more troubling is that many of these extinctions are not natural at all; they are closely linked to human behaviour. So, to me, this is not something we can simply shrug off. It should be a wake-up call.
components.chinese: 每當一個物種消失，地球其實就會變得更貧乏一些。在我看來，每一個物種都像生命之網中的一條線，所以只要其中一條被抽走，整個系統就會變得更脆弱、更容易失衡。更令人不安的是，很多這類絕種其實並不是自然發生的，而是和人類行為密切相關。所以，在我看來，這絕對不是一件可以輕輕帶過的事；它應該是一個讓人警醒的訊號。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Every time a species disappears, the planet becomes a little
poorer. In my view, each species is like a thread in the web of life, so when one is
removed, the whole system becomes weaker and more fragile. What makes it
even more troubling is that many of these extinctions are not natural at all; they
are closely linked to human behaviour. So, to me, this is not something we can
simply shrug off. It should be a wake-up call.
[每當一個物種消失，地球其實就會變得更貧乏一些。在我看來，每一個物種都像生命
之網中的一條線，所以只要其中一條被抽走，整個系統就會變得更脆弱、更容易失衡。
更令人不安的是，很多這類絕種其實並不是自然發生的，而是和人類行為密切相關。
所以，在我看來，這絕對不是一件可以輕輕帶過的事；它應該是一個讓人警醒的訊

components.english: For example, if pollinators, marine species, or forest animals disappear, the impact does not stop with those animals alone. It can spread to food production, ecosystems, and even human livelihoods. What looks like the loss of one species can, in reality, open the door to a chain reaction.
components.chinese: 例如，如果授粉動物、海洋物種，或者森林動物消失，影響並不只停留在那些動物本身。它還可能波及糧食生產、生態系統，甚至人類的生計。表面上看起來好像只是失去了一個物種，但實際上，它卻可能打開連鎖反應的大門。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if pollinators, marine species, or forest animals
disappear, the impact does not stop with those animals alone. It can spread to
food production, ecosystems, and even human livelihoods. What looks like the
loss of one species can, in reality, open the door to a chain reaction.
[例如，如果授粉動物、海洋物種，或者森林動物消失，影響並不只停留在那些動物本
身。它還可能波及糧食生產、生態系統，甚至人類的生計。表面上看起來好像只是失
去了一個物種，但實際上，它卻可能打開連鎖反應的大門。]

components.english: So overall, I think animal extinction is a very serious issue, because it reflects both environmental damage and human irresponsibility.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我認為動物絕種是一個非常嚴重的問題，因為它同時反映了環境破壞，以及人類的不負責任。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So overall, I think animal extinction is a very serious issue,
because it reflects both environmental damage and human irresponsibility.
[所以，整體來說，我認為動物絕種是一個非常嚴重的問題，因為它同時反映了環境破
壞，以及人類的不負責任。]

components.english: As for why it is happening, I think the biggest reason is human activity, especially habitat destruction, pollution, climate change, and illegal hunting or overexploitation.
components.chinese: 至於為甚麼會發生，我認為最大的原因就是人類活動，尤其是棲息地破壞、污染、氣候變化，以及非法捕獵或過度開發。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: As for why it is happening, I think the biggest reason is human
activity, especially habitat destruction, pollution, climate change, and illegal
hunting or overexploitation.
[至於為甚麼會發生，我認為最大的原因就是人類活動，尤其是棲息地破壞、污染、氣
候變化，以及非法捕獵或過度開發。]

components.english: The main problem is that animals are losing both space and safety. Forests are being cut down, oceans are being polluted, and temperatures are changing faster than many species can adapt. On top of that, some animals are still hunted for profit, status, or traditional products. In other words, human beings are squeezing wildlife from every direction, so it is no wonder that some species are on the brink.
components.chinese: 主要問題在於，動物正在同時失去生存空間和安全感。森林被砍伐，海洋受到污染，而氣溫變化的速度也比很多物種適應的速度更快。除此之外，有些動物仍然因為利益、炫耀心理，或者傳統產品需求而被獵殺。換句話說，人類正從四面八方擠壓野生動物的生存空間，所以有些物種走到瀕危邊緣，其實並不令人意外。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The main problem is that animals are losing both space and
safety. Forests are being cut down, oceans are being polluted, and temperatures
are changing faster than many species can adapt. On top of that, some animals
are still hunted for profit, status, or traditional products. In other words,
human beings are squeezing wildlife from every direction, so it is no wonder
that some species are on the brink.
[主要問題在於，動物正在同時失去生存空間和安全感。森林被砍伐，海洋受到污染，
而氣溫變化的速度也比很多物種適應的速度更快。除此之外，有些動物仍然因為利
益、炫耀心理，或者傳統產品需求而被獵殺。換句話說，人類正從四面八方擠壓野生動
物的生存空間，所以有些物種走到瀕危邊緣，其實並不令人意外。]

components.english: For instance, when land is cleared for roads, farming, or construction, animals may suddenly have nowhere to live or breed. In the same way, if rivers and seas are filled with waste, aquatic life pays the price. Little by little, those pressures add up until a species is pushed over the edge.
components.chinese: 例如，當土地被清理來興建道路、農地，或者各種建築時，動物可能會突然失去棲息和繁殖的地方。同樣地，如果河流和海洋充滿廢物，水中生物就會付出代價。這些壓力一點一滴累積起來，最後就可能把一個物種推過臨界點。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when land is cleared for roads, farming, or
construction, animals may suddenly have nowhere to live or breed. In the same
way, if rivers and seas are filled with waste, aquatic life pays the price. Little by
little, those pressures add up until a species is pushed over the edge.
[例如，當土地被清理來興建道路、農地，或者各種建築時，動物可能會突然失去棲息
和繁殖的地方。同樣地，如果河流和海洋充滿廢物，水中生物就會付出代價。這些壓力
一點一滴累積起來，最後就可能把一個物種推過臨界點。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say this is happening mainly because human progress has too often come at nature’s expense. Overall, if we fail to change our habits and protect wildlife more seriously, more species may vanish before our eyes.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說這種情況主要是因為人類的發展太常以犧牲自然為代價。如果我們不改變自己的習慣，並且更認真地保護野生動物，那麼將來可能會有更多物種在我們眼前消失。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say this is happening mainly because human
progress has too often come at nature’s expense. Overall, if we fail to change our
habits and protect wildlife more seriously, more species may vanish before our
eyes.
[總結來說，我會說這種情況主要是因為人類的發展太常以犧牲自然為代價。如果我
們不改變自己的習慣，並且更認真地保護野生動物，那麼將來可能會有更多物種在我
們眼前消失。]

question.english: Earlier people used animals for their work. Now, what do people use to do their work?
question.chinese: 以前人們會用動物來工作。現在，人們用甚麼來做他們的工作？

source_lines.text: Topic: Earlier people used animals for their work. Now, what do people use to
do their work?
以前人們會用動物來工作。現在，人們用甚麼來做他們的工作？

components.english: Nowadays, people mainly use machines, vehicles, and digital technology to do work that animals used to do in the past.
components.chinese: 現今，人們主要是使用機器、交通工具，以及數碼科技，去完成過去曾經依靠動物來做的工作。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Nowadays, people mainly use machines, vehicles, and digital technology
to do work that animals used to do in the past.
[現今，人們主要是使用機器、交通工具，以及數碼科技，去完成過去曾經依靠動物來
做的工作。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern tools are usually faster, stronger, and more efficient. In the past, people relied on horses, oxen, or donkeys to carry goods, plough fields, or travel long distances. Now, machines do most of the heavy lifting. Tractors have replaced farm animals in agriculture, trucks have replaced them in transport, and computers have transformed office work from the ground up. In other words, technology has taken the reins in many areas of life.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代工具通常更快、更強，而且效率更高。過去，人們會依靠馬、牛或驢子來運送貨物、耕田，或者長途移動。現在，機器已經承擔了大部分最吃重的工作。在農業方面，拖拉機取代了役用動物；在運輸方面，貨車取代了牠們；而在辦公工作上，電腦更是從根本上改變了工作方式。換句話說，科技在很多生活領域都已經接手主導位置。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that modern tools are usually faster,
stronger, and more efficient. In the past, people relied on horses, oxen, or
donkeys to carry goods, plough fields, or travel long distances. Now, machines
do most of the heavy lifting. Tractors have replaced farm animals in agriculture,
trucks have replaced them in transport, and computers have transformed office
work from the ground up. In other words, technology has taken the reins in
many areas of life.
[主要原因是，現代工具通常更快、更強，而且效率更高。過去，人們會依靠馬、牛或驢
子來運送貨物、耕田，或者長途移動。現在，機器已經承擔了大部分最吃重的工作。在
農業方面，拖拉機取代了役用動物；在運輸方面，貨車取代了牠們；而在辦公工作上，
電腦更是從根本上改變了工作方式。換句話說，科技在很多生活領域都已經接手主導
位置。]

components.english: For example, farmers used to use oxen to plough the land, whereas today they use tractors and other farm machinery. In the same way, goods that were once carried by horses or camels are now moved by vans, lorries, ships, and even planes. So, compared with the past, work today is much more machine-driven.
components.chinese: 例如，以前農夫會用牛來耕田，但今天他們會使用拖拉機和其他農業機械。同樣地，以前由馬或駱駝運送的貨物，現在則由客貨車、貨車、船隻，甚至飛機來運送。所以，和過去相比，今天的工作方式明顯更依賴機器。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, farmers used to use oxen to plough the land, whereas
today they use tractors and other farm machinery. In the same way, goods that
were once carried by horses or camels are now moved by vans, lorries, ships, and
even planes. So, compared with the past, work today is much more
machine-driven.
[例如，以前農夫會用牛來耕田，但今天他們會使用拖拉機和其他農業機械。同樣地，
以前由馬或駱駝運送的貨物，現在則由客貨車、貨車、船隻，甚至飛機來運送。所以，
和過去相比，今天的工作方式明顯更依賴機器。]

components.english: So, if I had to give a short answer, I would say people now use machinery and technology instead of animals for most kinds of work.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我簡單回答，我會說現代人已經以機械和科技取代動物，去完成大部分類型的工作。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to give a short answer, I would say people now use
machinery and technology instead of animals for most kinds of work.
[所以，如果要我簡單回答，我會說現代人已經以機械和科技取代動物，去完成大部分
類型的工作。]

components.english: On top of that, I think modern work depends not only on physical machines, but also on smart systems such as computers, software, and automation.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，現代工作所依賴的不只是實體機器，還包括電腦、軟件，以及自動化系統等智能工具。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think modern work depends not only on physical
machines, but also on smart systems such as computers, software, and
automation.
[除此之外，我也認為，現代工作所依賴的不只是實體機器，還包括電腦、軟件，以及
自動化系統等智能工具。]

components.english: In today’s world, a lot of work is no longer about muscle alone; it is about speed, accuracy, and coordination. That is why people use digital tools to organise tasks, analyse information, and communicate instantly. A machine may replace animal strength, but software often replaces time-consuming human effort as well. In that sense, modern work runs on both engines and algorithms. That said, animals have not disappeared from working life completely, because in some rural or mountainous areas, they are still useful.
components.chinese: 在今天這個世界，很多工作早已不再只是關乎體力；它更關乎速度、準確度，以及協調能力。這就是為甚麼人們會使用數碼工具來安排工作、分析資料，以及即時溝通。機器或許取代了動物的力量，而軟件則往往進一步取代了原本耗時的人力。在這個意義上，現代工作其實同時由引擎和演算法推動。不過，動物並沒有完全從工作世界中消失，因為在某些鄉村或山區地帶，牠們仍然有實際用途。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In today’s world, a lot of work is no longer about muscle alone;
it is about speed, accuracy, and coordination. That is why people use digital
tools to organise tasks, analyse information, and communicate instantly. A
machine may replace animal strength, but software often replaces
time-consuming human effort as well. In that sense, modern work runs on both
engines and algorithms. That said, animals have not disappeared from working
life completely, because in some rural or mountainous areas, they are still useful.
[在今天這個世界，很多工作早已不再只是關乎體力；它更關乎速度、準確度，以及協
調能力。這就是為甚麼人們會使用數碼工具來安排工作、分析資料，以及即時溝通。機
器或許取代了動物的力量，而軟件則往往進一步取代了原本耗時的人力。在這個意義
上，現代工作其實同時由引擎和演算法推動。不過，動物並沒有完全從工作世界中消
失，因為在某些鄉村或山區地帶，牠們仍然有實際用途。]

components.english: For instance, in an office, people now use laptops, cloud platforms, and artificial intelligence tools to finish tasks that once took far longer by hand. Meanwhile, in construction, cranes and excavators have replaced animal labour almost entirely. Even so, in some remote places, horses or mules may still be used to carry supplies where vehicles cannot easily go.
components.chinese: 例如，在辦公室裡，人們現在會使用手提電腦、雲端平台，以及人工智能工具，去完成那些以前用手處理需要花更長時間的工作。與此同時，在建築業中，起重機和挖掘機幾乎已經完全取代了動物勞力。即便如此，在一些偏遠地方，馬或騾子仍然可能被用來運送物資，因為那些地方車輛未必容易到達。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, in an office, people now use laptops, cloud platforms,
and artificial intelligence tools to finish tasks that once took far longer by hand.
Meanwhile, in construction, cranes and excavators have replaced animal labour
almost entirely. Even so, in some remote places, horses or mules may still be used
to carry supplies where vehicles cannot easily go.
[例如，在辦公室裡，人們現在會使用手提電腦、雲端平台，以及人工智能工具，去完
成那些以前用手處理需要花更長時間的工作。與此同時，在建築業中，起重機和挖掘
機幾乎已經完全取代了動物勞力。即便如此，在一些偏遠地方，馬或騾子仍然可能被
用來運送物資，因為那些地方車輛未必容易到達。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people now use machines, transport, and digital technology to do their work, while animals only play a limited role in special situations.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說現代人主要依靠機器、交通工具，以及數碼科技來工作，而動物只會在某些特殊情況下扮演有限角色。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people now use machines, transport, and digital
technology to do their work, while animals only play a limited role in special
situations.
[總結來說，我會說現代人主要依靠機器、交通工具，以及數碼科技來工作，而動物只
會在某些特殊情況下扮演有限角色。]

question.english: Research is being conducted on animals; is this a good idea?
question.chinese: 在動物身上進行研究，這是一個好主意嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Research is being conducted on animals; is this a good idea?
在動物身上進行研究，這是一個好主意嗎？

components.english: Personally, I think it is only a good idea in very limited cases, especially when the research is essential, tightly regulated, and there is no realistic alternative.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為這只有在非常有限的情況下才算是一個好主意，特別是當研究真的有必要、受到嚴格監管，而且沒有現實可行的替代方法時。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think it is only a good idea in very limited cases, especially
when the research is essential, tightly regulated, and there is no realistic
alternative.
[就我個人而言，我認為這只有在非常有限的情況下才算是一個好主意，特別是當研
究真的有必要、受到嚴格監管，而且沒有現實可行的替代方法時。]

components.english: The reason is that animal research sits at the crossroads of science and ethics. On the one hand, some medical studies may require scientists to see how a treatment affects a living body as a whole, so in that sense animal research can sometimes act as a stepping stone. On the other hand, animals are living creatures, not disposable tools, so their suffering cannot simply be brushed aside. To me, it is acceptable only as a last resort, not as the easy way out.
components.chinese: 原因是，動物研究其實處於科學與倫理的交叉點上。一方面，有些醫學研究的確需要科學家觀察某種治療如何影響一個完整的生命體，所以從這個角度來看，動物研究有時可以成為一塊踏腳石。另一方面，動物是有生命的生物，而不是可以隨便棄置的工具，因此牠們所承受的痛苦不能被輕輕帶過。在我看來，這種做法只有在作為最後手段時才可以被接受，而不應該成為圖方便的捷徑。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that animal research sits at the crossroads of
science and ethics. On the one hand, some medical studies may require scientists
to see how a treatment affects a living body as a whole, so in that sense animal
research can sometimes act as a stepping stone. On the other hand, animals are
living creatures, not disposable tools, so their suffering cannot simply be
brushed aside. To me, it is acceptable only as a last resort, not as the easy way
out.
[原因是，動物研究其實處於科學與倫理的交叉點上。一方面，有些醫學研究的確需要
科學家觀察某種治療如何影響一個完整的生命體，所以從這個角度來看，動物研究有
時可以成為一塊踏腳石。另一方面，動物是有生命的生物，而不是可以隨便棄置的工
具，因此牠們所承受的痛苦不能被輕輕帶過。在我看來，這種做法只有在作為最後手
段時才可以被接受，而不應該成為圖方便的捷徑。]

components.english: For example, if researchers are developing a treatment for a life-threatening disease and computer models or cell-based methods cannot yet provide enough reliable evidence, I can understand why limited animal research may still be used. In that kind of case, the goal is to save lives rather than to cut corners.
components.chinese: 例如，如果研究人員正在研發一種針對致命疾病的治療方法，而電腦模型或細胞測試仍然無法提供足夠可靠的證據，那麼我可以理解為甚麼有限度的動物研究仍然可能被使用。在這種情況下，目的在於拯救生命，而不是為了省事。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if researchers are developing a treatment for a
life-threatening disease and computer models or cell-based methods cannot yet
provide enough reliable evidence, I can understand why limited animal research
may still be used. In that kind of case, the goal is to save lives rather than to cut
corners.
[例如，如果研究人員正在研發一種針對致命疾病的治療方法，而電腦模型或細胞測
試仍然無法提供足夠可靠的證據，那麼我可以理解為甚麼有限度的動物研究仍然可能
被使用。在這種情況下，目的在於拯救生命，而不是為了省事。]

components.english: So, I would say it can be justified, but only under strict conditions and only when the purpose is truly important.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說這種做法在某些情況下是可以被合理化的，但前提是條件必須非常嚴格，而且研究目的必須真的十分重要。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I would say it can be justified, but only under strict
conditions and only when the purpose is truly important.
[所以，我會說這種做法在某些情況下是可以被合理化的，但前提是條件必須非常嚴
格，而且研究目的必須真的十分重要。]

components.english: That said, I do not think animal research is a good idea when it is used for trivial, commercial, or non-essential purposes.
components.chinese: 不過，如果動物研究是被用於瑣碎、商業化，或者並非必要的用途，我就不認為這是一個好主意。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think animal research is a good idea when it is used
for trivial, commercial, or non-essential purposes.
[不過，如果動物研究是被用於瑣碎、商業化，或者並非必要的用途，我就不認為這是
一個好主意。]

components.english: That is where I would draw the line. If the purpose is simply to improve a cosmetic product, increase sales, or gain a commercial advantage, then the moral cost is far too high. To be honest, it leaves a bad taste in my mouth when animals suffer for something that is clearly not a matter of life and death. On top of that, science is moving forward, so alternatives such as lab-grown tissues, simulations, and other non-animal methods should be pushed much harder. If better options are on the table, there is no excuse for turning a blind eye.
components.chinese: 那就是我會劃清界線的地方。如果目的是單純為了改良化妝品、提升銷量，或者取得商業優勢，那麼那種道德代價實在太高。老實說，如果動物要為一些明顯不是生死攸關的事情而受苦，這確實會令人很不舒服。再加上，科學本身也在不斷進步，所以像實驗室培養組織、模擬技術，以及其他非動物方法，都應該被更大力地推動。如果桌上已經有更好的選擇，那就沒有理由視而不見。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: That is where I would draw the line. If the purpose is simply
to improve a cosmetic product, increase sales, or gain a commercial advantage,
then the moral cost is far too high. To be honest, it leaves a bad taste in my
mouth when animals suffer for something that is clearly not a matter of life and
death. On top of that, science is moving forward, so alternatives such as
lab-grown tissues, simulations, and other non-animal methods should be pushed
much harder. If better options are on the table, there is no excuse for turning a
blind eye.
[那就是我會劃清界線的地方。如果目的是單純為了改良化妝品、提升銷量，或者取得
商業優勢，那麼那種道德代價實在太高。老實說，如果動物要為一些明顯不是生死攸
關的事情而受苦，這確實會令人很不舒服。再加上，科學本身也在不斷進步，所以像實
驗室培養組織、模擬技術，以及其他非動物方法，都應該被更大力地推動。如果桌上已
經有更好的選擇，那就沒有理由視而不見。]

components.english: For instance, if a company tests a new beauty product on animals simply to make it more marketable, I do not think that can really be defended. The benefit is relatively small, whereas the ethical cost is hard to ignore.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一家公司只是為了令一款新的美容產品更容易推銷，而在動物身上進行測試，我並不認為這種做法真的站得住腳。因為它所帶來的好處相對有限，但道德上的代價卻很難忽視。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a company tests a new beauty product on animals
simply to make it more marketable, I do not think that can really be defended.
The benefit is relatively small, whereas the ethical cost is hard to ignore.
[例如，如果一家公司只是為了令一款新的美容產品更容易推銷，而在動物身上進行
測試，我並不認為這種做法真的站得住腳。因為它所帶來的好處相對有限，但道德上
的代價卻很難忽視。]

components.english: Overall, I take a balanced view: animal research may be acceptable as a last resort in vital medical research, but beyond that, I do not think it is a good idea.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的看法比較平衡：如果是在關鍵性的醫學研究中，而且已經到了最後手段，那麼動物研究或許可以被接受；但除此之外，我並不認為這是一個好主意。
